title: malcolmia triloba subsp. broussonetii
intro: Malcolmia triloba subsp. broussonetii, also known as Broussonet's Whitlowgrass, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, as it contains compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and infections. The health benefits of Malcolmia triloba subsp. broussonetii include its ability to promote tissue repair, reduce inflammation, and prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi. Herbal preparations of this plant may be used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or topical creams and ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
